degree_class: LM-59 R - Scienze della comunicazione pubblica, d'impresa e pubblicità
admission_type: Restricted access / limited enrolment with admission test; non-EU applicants applying for a visa are evaluated on their academic CV and bachelor's degree score instead of sitting the admission test.
academic_requirements: Applicants must hold a bachelor's degree in one of the listed classes: L-20 Communication, L-18 Business administration, L-1 Cultural heritage, L-3 Visual arts, music, performing arts and fashion studies, L-5 Philosophy, L-6 Geography, L-10 Humanities, L-11 Modern languages and civilisations, L-12 Applied languages, L-14 Legal services, L-15 Tourism, L-16 Administration, L-19 Education and training, L-42 History, L-31 Computer science, L-24 Psychology, L-33 Economics, L-36 Political sciences and international relations, L-37 Peace studies, L-39 Social work, or L-40 Sociology, or an equivalent class under the relevant Italian ministerial decrees. Applicants with a different degree class may still apply if they already earned at least 50 ECTS in the academic fields listed in the call, including philosophy/theory of language, linguistics, modern and contemporary history, computer science/information processing, sociology of culture/communication/general/political/economic-labour/environmental processes, political science, English language, contemporary art/theatre/cinema, Italian literature, political economy/economic policy/applied economics/business/management/organisation, statistics/social statistics, private/commercial/administrative/constitutional/public/international/EU law, and general/social/work-and-organisational psychology. An equivalent foreign qualification recognized as suitable by the University is accepted. The call is also open to students in the final year of a first-cycle programme in Italy or abroad, provided they graduate by 31 December 2026. The 2026/2027 manifesto states that 180 credits are required for admission.
language_requirements: English proficiency at CEFR B2 or higher is required for admission. UniMi accepts three official routes: a recognized B2-or-higher certificate issued no more than 3 years before the admission application and uploaded with the application; a SLAM-certified English level achieved during a University of Milan degree programme no more than 4 years before the application date, checked automatically; or the SLAM English entry test scheduled for 24 September 2026. Applicants who do not submit a valid certificate or do not meet the required level are directed to the SLAM test during the admission procedure. If they do not pass the test, they must meet the language requirement by 31 December 2026; the programme page and the UniMi SLAM page state that recognized certificates must be submitted to SLAM via InformaStudenti by the programme deadline. For non-EU applicants applying for a visa, the call additionally states that enrolment is possible only if they pass the Italian language proficiency test, except in cases of exemption. The programme is not fully English at programme level: UniMi lists the overall teaching language as Italian and English.
application_deadline_eu: Italian, EU residents and equivalent applicants not applying for a visa: application from 24 March 2026 to 25 August 2026 at 2:00pm; admission test on 2 September 2026 at 9:30am; ranking list publication on 8 September 2026 at 2:00pm; enrolment from 8 September to 15 September 2026 at 2:00pm.
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU applicants applying for a visa: application from 24 March 2026 to 29 May 2026; ranking list publication by 11 June 2026 at 2:00pm; enrolment from 11 June to 18 June 2026 at 2:00pm; first update and enrolment deadline from 19 June 2026 at 2:00pm to 2 July 2026 at 2:00pm; second update and enrolment deadline from 3 July to 15 July 2026 at 2:00pm; Universitaly pre-enrolment must be submitted by 31 July 2026.
required_documents: For applicants with an Italian qualification: self-certification of degree/enrolment with exam records; for graduates or graduands from other Italian universities this must include the exams taken, grades, ECTS credits, and disciplinary scientific sectors.
Recognized English B2-or-higher certificate, if the applicant uses the certificate route for the English requirement.
For applicants with foreign qualifications at application stage: bachelor's degree (B.Sc. or equivalent) or degree-awarding certificate, in Italian, English, French, German, or Spanish.

For applicants with foreign qualifications at application stage: diploma supplement or, alternatively, transcript of records including courses, marks, ECTS, and academic field, in English, Italian, French, German, or Spanish.
Curriculum vitae for non-EU applicants applying for a visa.
For non-Italian candidates with foreign qualifications: the passport pages containing personal information, or other valid ID document.

For applicants still completing the first-cycle degree with foreign qualifications: transcript of records plus a document listing exams already passed but not yet registered, exams not yet taken, and the expected graduation date.
For enrolment finalization with a foreign qualification: legalized or apostilled bachelor's degree (or equivalent), unless legalization or apostille is exempt by law.
For enrolment finalization with a foreign qualification: high school diploma.
For enrolment finalization with a foreign qualification: official Italian translation of academic qualifications, unless the originals are in English, French, German, or Spanish.

For enrolment finalization with a foreign qualification: Statement of Comparability from an ENIC-NARIC centre such as CIMEA, or a Dichiarazione di Valore; for Lisbon Convention countries, the Diploma Supplement or ARDI correspondence statement may replace these as stated by UniMi.
For enrolment finalization with a foreign qualification: Italian tax code (codice fiscale).
For non-EU enrolment finalization: receipt of the residence-permit application or a valid residence permit.
entry_exam_or_test: For Italian, EU and equivalent applicants not applying for a visa, admission includes one test on 2 September 2026 at 9:30am assessing communication theory, computer science, marketing, statistics, and current events. Each correct answer scores 1 point and each wrong or missing answer scores 0. The final ranking weights degree mark or grade point average at 40%, communication theory at 25%, computer science at 10%, marketing at 10%, statistics at 10%, and current events at 5%. During the test, candidates may not use books, notes, calculators, smartphones, or similar devices. The call also provides suggested handbooks in both Italian and English. For non-EU applicants applying for a visa, admission is based on evaluation by an Admission Board of the academic CV and bachelor's degree score in lieu of the admission test.

Public and Corporate Communication (COM) - 2 curricula in English

Non-EU Candidates Applying for a Visa ... Application from 24 March to 29 May 2026
Admission to the Master Degree Programme requires candidates to hold a degree in the following degree classes
at least 50 ECTS in the following academic fields

Self-certification of degree/enrolment with exam records
Bachelor’s Degree ... Diploma supplement ... Passport ... Curriculum vitae
To finalize the online enrolment ... applicants with foreign qualifications must submit the required documents
For admission, students must take a test aimed at assessing their expertise in the fields of IT, marketing, statistics, current affairs and communication theories.
The degree programme in Public and Corporate Communication reserves 30 places for non-EU applicants who require a study visa.
The student must obtain 9 CFU in one of the following modules.

uncertainty_notes: No required field remained uncertain after checking the A.Y. 2026/2027 UniMi programme page, the official 2026/2027 call PDF, the 2026/2027 manifesto, the UniMi programmes-in-English page, and the UniMi SLAM English-requirements page on 2026-06-11.

The programme is officially mixed-language rather than fully English. UniMi labels the overall programme language as Italian and English, and separately lists COM as having 2 curricula in English. Cross-checking the 2026/2027 manifesto/course list shows Corporate Communication and Digital Media, Data and Society as English-taught curricula, while Communication and Marketing is mainly Italian with some English-taught options and labs.
